title: Deuteronomy: Worship and Justice
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of deuteronomy: worship and justice and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study deuteronomy: worship and justice and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: Where did Deuteronomy command Israel to bring all their sacrifices and offerings? Answer: to the one place the LORD your God will choose — the central sanctuary.
segments.correct_answer: to the place the LORD your God will choose — the central sanctuary
acceptable_variants: the centralized place of worship God would designate
one sanctuary chosen by God — not wherever they please
centralization of worship at the place god would choose

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: Deuteronomy 12's command to offer sacrifices only at the place God would choose; this curbed local high places and ultimately pointed to Jerusalem and the temple
A tenth of agricultural produce and animals commanded in Deuteronomy 14; portions went to the Levites, the central sanctuary feast, and the poor in alternating years
